evidence_quotes.quote: from when I was considering a game in which I set my hours of play to be uneconomical and compelled to buy a remastered or completely new version.
simply selling the same concept again at full price after x years, and giving me the possibility of refusing the version already bought, which would have had to be fired, is not fair!

paragraphs.text: I am happy to pay money (with the 19 % tax) for my free work, but I do not see it as one of what will be imposed on me from when I was considering a game in which I set my hours of play to be uneconomical and compelled to buy a remastered or completely new version. Some with more bows than in the old game, or the x-year old bows were not fixed... I also play a tiktakto, not because of new graphics or colour reshaping, but because of the game, simply selling the same concept again at full price after x years, and giving me the possibility of refusing the version already bought, which would have had to be fired, is not fair!
